title: Hemionitis harrisii
intro: Hemionitis harrisii, also known as Harris's fern or Japanese lady fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and acting as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis harrisii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis and coughs.
